sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Audrey King to give Holmes-Hunter Lecture
feed.summary: Audrey King, senior vice president of corporate responsibility for Georgia Power and president and CEO of The Georgia Power Foundation, will deliver the 2026 Holmes-Hunter Lecture at the University of Georgia Chapel. The annual lecture, honoring UGA's first Black students, Charlayne Hunter-Gault and Hamilton Holmes Sr., recognizes King's extensive career and leadership in corporate responsibility and community engagement across Georgia.

feed.title: Billion-dollar Settlement Resolves Honeywell, Flexjet Dispute
feed.summary: Honeywell and Flexjet have reached a billion-dollar settlement, resolving a dispute that stemmed from a breach of engine maintenance agreement alleged in 2023. As part of the agreement, the fractional-share operator Flexjet will also extend its existing maintenance service plan with Honeywell.

feed.title: YPF-Pluspetrol asset swap shifts Vaca Muerta landscape
feed.summary: Argentine hydrocarbons companies YPF and Pluspetrol have agreed to an asset swap in the Vaca Muerta shale formation, which will see Pluspetrol acquire a 44.4% stake in Vaca Muerta Inversiones (VMI) and YPF take full control of the Las Tacanas, Meseta Buena Esperanza, and Aguada Villanueva areas. The deal involves significant oil and gas producing blocks and will consolidate YPF's position in several key areas. The transaction highlights continued activity in the Vaca Muerta region, with other related developments including a local supplier registry in Río Negro province and ongoing projects like the Vaca Muerta Sur oil export pipeline and several LNG initiatives.

feed.title: After Decades of Deflection, ExxonMobil Moves to Reshape Global Climate Accounting
feed.summary: ExxonMobil is backing a new initiative called Carbon Measures, which seeks to change global climate accounting by shifting pollution liabilities from producers to consumers. Critics, including BankTrack, argue this system is a "transparent delay tactic" that undermines existing reporting frameworks like the Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Protocol, which most companies currently use. ExxonMobil has also filed a lawsuit against California's climate disclosure laws, asserting that fully reporting emissions via the GHG Protocol violates its free speech rights.

feed.title: Mike West calls it a career
feed.summary: Mike West recently retired after a 30-year career at Broadridge, where he served as a marketing leader involved in significant international growth. He reflected on industry changes, career highlights interacting with clients and colleagues, and challenges that spurred continuous learning. West plans to focus on arts, humanities, fitness, and spending more time with family in his retirement.

feed.title: Above the Noise: Look beyond distracting headlines
feed.summary: Invesco's Chief Global Market Strategist, Brian Levitt, argues investors should look past distracting headlines and noise, emphasizing a bullish stance on stocks due to broadening markets, despite geopolitical maneuvering and concerns over Fed independence. He suggests that investors will gradually diversify away from US assets, leading to continued US dollar weakness and potential outperformance of international assets in 2026. The article also touches on the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence, despite challenges and regulation needs, highlighting its impact on fields like medical research.

feed.title: The Truth About MarketAxess: Is This Quiet Fintech Power Player Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: This article examines MarketAxess (MKTX), a fintech platform dominating electronic bond trading, contrasting its quiet but powerful market presence with popular meme stocks. It evaluates MarketAxess's business model, price performance, and competitive landscape against Tradeweb, concluding that it's a valuable long-term investment for those focused on quality infrastructure rather than short-term viral hype. The article advises potential investors to consider its steady transaction revenue and niche dominance in the bond market.

feed.title: Simon Property Moved to Terminate Two Saks Global Leases Just Before Bankruptcy
feed.summary: Just prior to Saks Global's bankruptcy filing, landlord Simon Property Group moved to terminate two leases held by Saks' Neiman Marcus division and another Saks lease, citing over $7 million in unpaid rent. Simon Property Group is seeking court confirmation of the lease terminations and argues that Saks Global has no legal right to remain in the properties. This action comes after Simon Property Group invested significantly in Saks Global's acquisition of Neiman Marcus Group.

feed.title: J.P. Morgan Identifies Textron (TXT) as a Top Short Idea for 202
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan has identified Textron Inc. (TXT) as a top short idea for Q1 2026, primarily due to anticipated challenges in its Aviation segment, including a Q4 shortfall in Cessna deliveries and increased capital expenditure for the MV-75 program. Despite consistent revenue growth and stable profitability, Textron faces concerns regarding its valuation, with P/E, P/S, and P/B ratios near 3-year highs, suggesting potential overvaluation. The company's Altman Z-Score indicates some financial stress, and its ROIC is below WACC, highlighting inefficiencies in capital allocation.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Inc.: How a “Software-Like” Steel Platform Is Rewiring Heavy Industry
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics Inc. is redefining the steel industry by acting like a product startup, utilizing electric-arc furnace (EAF) mini-mills, scrap recycling, and downstream fabrication to create a differentiated product platform. This approach allows them to offer targeted steel products with the responsiveness of a SaaS vendor, outcompeting legacy steel giants. The company's strategy emphasizes lower costs, a better environmental profile, and value-added products, positioning it as a resilient and attractive investment in a cyclical industry.

feed.title: General Electric Co (NYSE:GE) Slowing Growth
feed.summary: General Electric (NYSE:GE) shares dipped despite strong Q4 2025 earnings that beat analyst estimates for profit and revenue. The decline was attributed to investor concerns over slowing growth in key segments like commercial engines and a moderated 2026 outlook. While the company reported robust free cash flow and increased orders, its full-year 2026 guidance projects slower adjusted revenue growth compared to 2025.

feed.title: TD Cowen Upgrades Fortinet (FTNT) to 'Buy' | FTNT Stock News
feed.summary: TD Cowen has upgraded Fortinet (FTNT) from a "Hold" to a "Buy" rating, maintaining a price target of $100.00 USD. This upgrade comes amidst varied analyst opinions, with other firms like Citigroup and Piper Sandler maintaining "Neutral" ratings and Morgan Stanley and JP Morgan holding "Underweight" ratings on the cybersecurity vendor. Based on analyst forecasts, the average target price for FTNT is $86.86, suggesting a 4.41% upside from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $104.87, indicating a 26.06% upside.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh Investigates Shareholder Rights Violations at Diamond Hill, Generation Bio, and Contango
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating potential shareholder rights violations at Diamond Hill Investment Group (DHIL), Generation Bio Co. (GBIO), and Contango ORE, Inc. (CTGO). The investigations concern various transactions including DHIL's sale to First Eagle Investments, GBIO's sale to XOMA Royalty Corporation, and CTGO's merger with Dolly Varden Silver Corporation. The law firm is offering free legal consultations to affected shareholders to help them understand their rights and potential compensation opportunities.

feed.title: Smart Money Is Betting Big In TMUS Options
feed.summary: Large investors have taken a predominantly bearish stance on T-Mobile US (NASDAQ: TMUS) through options trading, with 70% of 10 uncommon options trades being bearish. These significant trades suggest an expectation of price movement within the $180.0 to $200.0 range for TMUS over the next three months. The article advises retail traders to be aware of this "smart money" activity, as it often precedes significant stock movements.

feed.title: Invitation Homes Expands Southeast Footprint With $89 Million Acquisition of Atlanta-based ResiBuilt
feed.summary: Invitation Homes has acquired Atlanta-based build-to-rent developer ResiBuilt Homes for $89 million, expanding its presence in the Southeast and adding 23 existing contracts and options on 1,500 lots. This acquisition is expected to be accretive to 2026 AFFO per share. Additionally, Crow Holdings plans a 245MW data center in Fort Worth, Solidion Technologies received a third federal grant for battery research, and HALL Arts Hotel joined Marriott Bonvoy's Autograph Collection.

feed.title: Caterpillar Expands Autonomous Technology Capabilities
feed.summary: Caterpillar is expanding its autonomous technology capabilities across a wide range of construction equipment, including excavators, loaders, haul trucks, dozers, and compactors. This expansion builds on more than three decades of development in automation and machine intelligence, utilizing AI, machine learning, computer vision, and various sensors. The company's autonomous mining fleet has already moved over 11 billion metric tons of material, and Caterpillar plans to showcase its latest autonomy developments at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026.

feed.title: Flash US PMI signals business growth in lower gear at start of 2026
feed.summary: The January flash US PMI indicates that while economic growth continues, the pace has cooled compared to late last year, signaling an annualized GDP growth of 1.5% for December and January. New business and jobs growth are subdued, with near-stagnant payroll numbers and a dip in future optimism due to concerns over political environment, high costs, and tariffs. Input costs remain elevated, leading to continued rises in selling prices for goods and services.

feed.title: Fortinet in focus as TD Cowen upgrades on back of 'solid checks' (FTNT:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: TD Cowen has upgraded Fortinet's shares to "Buy" based on "solid checks" suggesting stability for fiscal year 2026. This upgrade caused Fortinet's shares to jump 7% in late morning trading. The cybersecurity company has been put in the spotlight by this investment firm's positive outlook.

feed.title: Dominion Energy Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.6675 a Share, Payable March 20 to Shareholders of Record as of Feb. 27
feed.summary: Dominion Energy (D) announced that its board of directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.6675 per share of common stock. This dividend is payable on March 20, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026.

feed.title: Analysts Estimate Franklin Resources (BEN) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
feed.summary: Analysts project Franklin Resources (BEN) to report a year-over-year decline in earnings and revenues for the quarter ending December 2025. The company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.56 per share on revenues of $2.15 billion. The Zacks Earnings ESP indicates a potential earnings miss, as the Most Accurate Estimate is lower than the Zacks Consensus Estimate.

feed.title: Northern Trust Analysts Boost Their Forecasts After Upbeat Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Northern Trust (NASDAQ: NTRS) reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings with adjusted EPS of $2.69 and sales of $2.123 billion, surpassing market estimates. Following these upbeat results, several analysts raised their price targets for the stock despite a slight dip in share price. Evercore ISI Group, Wells Fargo, and RBC Capital all maintained their ratings and increased their price targets for Northern Trust.

feed.title: Baird Adjusts Price Target on Tractor Supply to $61 From $67, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Baird has adjusted its price target for Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) from $67 to $61, while retaining an Outperform rating on the stock. This update reflects a change in the analyst's valuation projection for the specialty retailer. The article also lists other recent analyst recommendations for Tractor Supply, most of which have also lowered their price targets.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share for $345
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant shareholder in Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), recently purchased a single share of its common stock for $345.78, bringing its total direct ownership to 3,487,697 shares. This transaction follows a period of notable developments for Texas Pacific Land, including a Q3 2025 earnings miss, a strategic partnership with Bolt Data & Energy, and a three-for-one stock split that became effective in December 2025. KeyBanc also initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, recognizing the company's substantial land and royalty interests.

feed.title: Arista Networks Deepens AI Role With Anthropic Ties And VeloCloud Deal
feed.summary: Arista Networks is emerging as a significant player in AI infrastructure, bolstered by a partnership with Anthropic and the acquisition of VeloCloud. These strategic moves are positioning Arista to capitalize on the growing demand for AI-ready data centers and expanding its enterprise networking solutions. The company's stock performance and analyst forecasts reflect optimism about its future in the AI sector, though insider selling and integration challenges are noted as potential risks.

feed.title: Does Enphase (ENPH) Expanding GaN Microinverters Reshape Its Commercial Solar Competitive Edge?
feed.summary: Enphase Energy has launched US production shipments of its IQ9N-3P commercial microinverter, utilizing gallium nitride technology for three-phase 480V commercial solar projects. This expands Enphase's offerings into higher-voltage commercial markets, potentially improving efficiency and supporting domestic content rules. While this product adds a fresh catalyst, Enphase's near-term outlook still relies on order momentum, margins, and inventory management, with risks including execution in commercial, policy volatility, and share price swings.

feed.title: CVRx’s stock up as heart failure device to be trialled in expanded patient group
feed.summary: CVRx's stock rose significantly after the company announced its Barostim heart failure device would be trialled in an expanded patient population through the BENEFIT-HF study. If successful, this trial could triple the indicated patient population for Barostim, broadening access to this neuromodulation-based therapy. The device is already approved in the US and Europe for specific heart failure patient groups and aims to improve symptoms by rebalancing the autonomic nervous system.

feed.title: Fortinet Stock Rises After Upgrade on AI Outlook
feed.summary: Fortinet stock saw a significant rise after TD Cowen upgraded its rating to Buy from Hold, setting a $100 price target. The upgrade comes amidst an analyst's view that negative sentiments surrounding the cybersecurity firm are unwarranted, leading to its strongest daily performance in almost a year. The article highlights the importance of not succumbing to the "AI Is Eating Software" narrative.

feed.title: Does RBC’s IVZ Upgrade and QQQ Reclassification Alter Invesco’s Active–Passive Growth Balance?
feed.summary: Invesco has garnered attention due to an RBC Capital upgrade, stronger organic growth predictions, and expected margin expansion through 2026–2027, along with benefits from the QQQ ETF reclassification. The company is also realigning its global operations by partnering with CI Global Asset Management in Canada. While the stock has seen a significant 1-year return, its current price near analyst targets raises the bar for future positive surprises and sensitivity to market fluctuations.

feed.title: Stronger Earnings, Buybacks and Comerica Deal Progress Could Be A Game Changer For Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB)
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) recently announced strong Q4 2025 results with increased net interest income and net income, stable credit costs, and a completed US$300 million buyback. The bank also reported progress on its Comerica acquisition and a leadership transition in its chief credit officer role. These factors, alongside positive 2026 net interest income guidance, are shaping a favorable investment narrative for the regional bank.

feed.title: Fidelity National Adds AI Leader Anil Chakravarthy As Investors Weigh Valuation
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) has appointed Anil Chakravarthy to its Board of Directors, a move aligning with FIS's strategic focus on integrating enterprise software, cloud transformation, and artificial intelligence into financial services. While not a direct financial signal, this appointment signifies a governance alignment with technology-driven changes, which may influence future product direction and capital allocation. Investors are encouraged to monitor FIS's discussions on AI, cloud migration, and capital allocation in upcoming presentations, especially considering its current P/E of 179.3 and forward P/E of 21.9.

feed.title: Over 31,000 bank customers give Fifth Third top score in USA Today study
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank (Nasdaq: FITB) has received a 5-star rating in USA Today’s inaugural America’s Best Customer Service for Financial Services study, based on feedback from over 31,000 U.S. customers. The recognition highlights the bank's commitment to personalized experiences and customer satisfaction across various service dimensions. This achievement validates the company's vision and ongoing investments in enhancing customer experiences, including digital tools and a planned integration with Comerica later this year.

feed.title: FFIV Gears Up to Report Q1 Earnings: What's in the Cards?
feed.summary: F5 Inc. (FFIV) is set to report its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results on January 27, with analysts expecting non-GAAP EPS between $3.35-$3.85 and revenues between $730 million and $780 million. The company's product segment, particularly software and systems, is anticipated to show strong performance driven by demand for hybrid multicloud architecture, system refreshes, and AI readiness. F5 also appears to have effectively managed a recent data breach, with minimal impact on critical systems.

feed.title: Bill Ackman's Alphabet Bet Is Crushing It: Here's How Much He's Made Since Q3
feed.summary: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management has seen significant gains on its Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG, NASDAQ: GOOGL) stake, initially acquired in Q1 2023. Since the end of Q3 2025, the fund's Alphabet holdings have increased in value by over $2 billion, reaching new all-time highs. Despite trimming some Class A shares, Ackman's bet on the undervalued tech giant has yielded substantial returns, with investors awaiting his next 13F filing for potential further changes.

feed.title: American Express (AXP) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: American Express (AXP) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. Despite a slight downward revision in the consensus EPS estimate, the stock carries a Zacks Rank #3, making a conclusive prediction difficult regarding an earnings beat. However, investors are advised to consider other factors alongside the Earnings ESP and Zacks Rank.

feed.title: DA Davidson lowers Broadridge Financial stock price target to $228
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reduced its price target for Broadridge Financial (NYSE:BR) from $240.00 to $228.00, maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm cited compression in average peer multiples and a slight reduction in second-quarter projections for event-driven proxy distribution activity as reasons for the adjustment. Despite the lowered target, Broadridge’s stock is considered undervalued based on InvestingPro data and has a history of 19 consecutive years of dividend increases.

feed.title: Pfizer Advances New COPD Candidate Into Phase 2/3, Adding Depth to Its Post-COVID Pipeline
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) has initiated a phase 2/3 clinical trial (NCT07363694) for its new drug candidate, PF-07275315, aimed at adults with moderate to severe Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD). This interventional study will evaluate the drug's effectiveness in reducing flare-ups and improving lung function through a series of under-the-skin injections against a placebo. The program is significant for Pfizer as it works to diversify its revenue streams post-COVID, potentially entering a large market currently dominated by rivals like GSK and AstraZeneca.

feed.title: Ivalua Again Named a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Source-to-Pay Suites
feed.summary: Ivalua, a global leader in spend management, has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner "Magic Quadrant for Source-to-Pay Suites." This recognition is based on Ivalua's strong ability to execute and completeness of vision, as evaluated against 13 software vendors. The report highlights the increasing importance of Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions for organizations seeking to boost efficiency, reduce costs, and optimize supplier strategies, especially with the growing integration of Agentic AI capabilities.

feed.title: Fermi America hit with class action lawsuit
feed.summary: Fermi America, a company proposing an 11GW data center campus in Texas, has been hit with a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors who purchased securities between October 1 and December 11, 2025. The lawsuit follows the withdrawal of an anchor tenant, which caused Fermi's share price to drop over 33 percent. The company, co-founded by Rick Perry, aims to build an 18 million sq ft facility despite having no operational data centers.

feed.title: P&G prioritizes data, AI to tackle fragmented ‘new media reality’
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble is leveraging data and AI to navigate the increasingly fragmented media landscape, aiming to redefine its brand-building strategies. With retailers becoming media platforms and a non-linear consumer path to purchase, P&G CEO Shailesh Jejurikar emphasized the company's "enormous wealth of consumer data" and AI capabilities to uncover insights for product innovation and marketing campaigns across various channels. Despite sluggish U.S. sales, P&G expects sales growth between 1% to 5% for its fiscal 2026 year.

feed.title: Slideshow: New products from Danone North America, The Coca-Cola Co. and Koia
feed.summary: This article highlights new functional beverage products from Danone North America, The Coca-Cola Co., and Koia. Danone's Silk brand is introducing a plant-based protein milk with less sugar and added fiber. Coca-Cola is launching BodyArmor Flash I.V. with caffeine and electrolytes, while Koia is debuting a protein soda line with plant-based protein and prebiotic fiber.

feed.title: Dollar Tree stock slips toward $125 as BNP Paribas Exane turns bearish on DLTR
feed.summary: Dollar Tree shares dropped following a downgrade by BNP Paribas Exane, which cited weaker sales momentum and diminishing benefits from multi-price items. Analyst Chris Bottiglieri slashed the price target to $87 from $118, noting tougher year-over-year comparisons due to past price hikes and softer demand for consumables. Investors are now keenly awaiting late-March earnings to assess store traffic, margins, and the future impact of the company's strategies.

feed.title: Zip Named a Visionary in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Source-to-Pay Suites – The Only Agentic Procurement Orchestration Platform to Make the List
feed.summary: Zip, an enterprise AI procurement orchestration platform, has been recognized as a Visionary in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Source-to-Pay Suites, becoming the youngest company and only agentic procurement orchestration platform to achieve this. Founded in 2020, Zip has rapidly distinguished itself among established players by offering an end-to-end source-to-pay platform where AI agents automate procurement workflows, leading to over $6 billion in customer savings. This achievement highlights a shift towards orchestration as essential for modern procurement.

feed.title: Insulet Stock Is Going Off: Game-Changer Buy Or Overhyped Trap?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Insulet (PODD), the company behind the Omnipod tubeless insulin pump, examining whether it's a worthwhile investment or an overhyped stock. It highlights Insulet's strong product loyalty, recurring revenue model, and the growing demand driven by the global diabetes problem, while also considering its market volatility and competition. The author concludes that Insulet is a selective buy for long-term investors comfortable with growth stock dynamics and a real-world health mission.

feed.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Issues Positive Forecast for Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) Stock Price
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has increased its price target for Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) from $139 to $159, maintaining an "outperform" rating and implying a 6.26% upside. This positive outlook follows Northern Trust's strong Q4 earnings beat, with EPS of $2.42 and revenue of $3.15 billion, driven by record net interest income and a declared quarterly dividend of $0.80. Despite a mixed analyst consensus (Hold rating with an average target of $141.77), the company's robust financial performance and management's raised targets are key catalysts for the positive forecast, though concerns about elevated operating costs and valuation persist.

feed.title: Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) Given New $228.00 Price Target at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson lowered its price target for Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) to $228 from $240, maintaining a "neutral" rating despite the company beating quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. Broadridge reported $1.51 EPS on $1.59 billion revenue and provided FY2026 EPS guidance of $9.230-$9.580. The report also highlighted recent insider selling by a VP and the president, while institutional investors hold approximately 90% of the stock.

feed.title: GE Aerospace Stock Outlook: Is the Path to $498 a Buy?
feed.summary: GE Aerospace, now a pure-play aviation company, is positioned for growth driven by its large installed engine base and high-margin aftermarket services. A valuation model projects the stock to reach $498 by December 2029, implying a 12% annualized return from its current price of $318, based on strong revenue growth and expanding net income margins. The company's "FLIGHT DECK" lean operating model and the maturation of the LEAP engine program are expected to fuel profitability.

feed.title: Google stock price today: Alphabet GOOG Class C ticks up as judge keeps search antitrust suit alive
feed.summary: Alphabet's non-voting Class C shares (GOOG) saw a slight increase after a U.S. judge allowed a consumer antitrust lawsuit against Google regarding its search dominance to proceed. The stock's movement also comes as analysts set a $400 target on Alphabet ahead of its early-February earnings report, with increasing bets on cloud and AI driving future growth despite regulatory pressures. The legal challenge focuses on Google's default search engine placement, which coincides with the company's significant investment in AI-powered search features.

feed.title: Coupa Named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Source-to-Pay Suites
feed.summary: Coupa has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Source-to-Pay (S2P) Suites for the third consecutive time, achieving the highest position for Ability to Execute. This recognition highlights Coupa's AI platform for total spend management, which unifies procurement, finance, and supply chain processes to deliver evidence-based intelligence, effortless total cost of ownership, and a strong network effect for both buyers and suppliers. The company continues to evolve its platform, helping organizations like Nationwide Building Society transform their procurement and achieve measurable value.

feed.title: GUESS taps Chiara Ferragni as face of Spring campaign
feed.summary: GUESS has announced that global entrepreneur and fashion icon Chiara Ferragni will be the star of its Spring 2026 worldwide advertising campaign. Shot by the Morelli Brothers in Lugano, Switzerland, this collaboration marks Ferragni's return to the brand after 13 years and aims to launch a new chapter for GUESS, blending its iconic heritage with a contemporary, social-first approach. The campaign and collection will emphasize femininity, independence, and self-expression, featuring the new Camden Bag and launching globally in February.

feed.title: Sixth straight ISG Leader ranking spotlights Dynatrace AI observability
feed.summary: Dynatrace has been recognized as a Leader for the sixth consecutive year in the 2025 ISG Provider Lens® Multi Public Cloud Solutions report, specifically for both Cloud-Native Observability and Cloud-Native Security. This recognition highlights Dynatrace's AI-powered platform which provides intelligent analytics for proactive security and streamlined cloud operations. The company's strong partnerships and innovation in unifying security and observability with agentic SRE integrations were key factors in their continued leadership position.
